2606.24790.title: Grad Detect: Gradient-Based Hallucination Detection in LLMs
2606.24790.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet they remain prone to generating hallucinations. Detecting these hallucinations is critical for deploying LLMs reliably in high-stakes applications. We present Grad Detect, a gradient-based approach for predicting hallucinations by analyzing layer-wise gradient patterns from a single forward-backward pass during inference. Our method shows that the internal gradient structure of a model carries rich information about the correctness of its output. This information is not accessible through output-level signals alone. We evaluate Grad Detect on several Q&A benchmarks across both hallucination detection and model abstention prediction, where it consistently outperforms confidence-based and sampling-based baselines. Through comprehensive layer ablation studies across all eleven models from four architectural families, we find that the final five layers concentrate over 97% of the discriminative gradient signal, enabling efficient deployment with minimal performance loss. Grad Detect provides a unified framework for predicting multiple dimensions of LLM reliability, offering strong predictive performance alongside interpretable insights into where and how model failures originate.
2606.24790.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在多樣任務中展示了卓越的能力，但它們仍然容易生成幻覺。檢測這些幻覺對於在高風險應用中可靠地部署LLMs至關重要。我們提出了Grad Detect，一種基於梯度的方法，通過分析推理過程中單次前向-反向傳播的層級梯度模式來預測幻覺。我們的方法顯示，模型的內部梯度結構攜帶了有關其輸出正確性的豐富信息。這些信息僅通過輸出級信號是無法獲得的。我們在多個問答基準上評估Grad Detect，涵蓋幻覺檢測和模型放棄預測，在這些基準中，它始終優於基於置信度和基於採樣的基線。通過對來自四個架構系列的所有十一個模型進行全面的層級消融研究，我們發現最後五層集中超過97%的判別梯度信號，從而實現高效部署，並且性能損失最小。Grad Detect提供了一個統一的框架，用於預測LLM可靠性的多個維度，提供強大的預測性能以及對模型失敗來源的可解釋見解。
2606.24790.author: Anand Kamat et.al.
2606.24790.authors: Anand Kamat, Daniel Blake, Brent M. Werness
